Tempura batter is wheat flour — contains gluten.
Wheat-flour batter — contains gluten.
Vegan fried 'chicken' — coating typically uses wheat flour; contains gluten unless confirmed otherwise.
Tofu is usually dusted in potato starch (GF) but the dashi/sauce contains soy sauce (wheat) — ask for a tamari-based or salt-only version.
Wheat pasta — contains gluten. Contains alliums.
Wheat cake flour — contains gluten.
Parfait may include granola/cake/cookie pieces — ask which toppings are wheat-free.

items.ja: 日替わりおばんざいランチ（御山定食）
items.note: Main dish + miso soup + three small dishes + pickles. Ask for fish/veg grilled or dressed with salt (塩) not soy sauce/dashi; confirm miso soup dashi. Request a fish- and dashi-free plate for vegan.

items.ja: 黒毛和牛ステーキ定食
items.note: Ask for the steak seasoned with salt and pepper only (塩), no tare/soy-sauce glaze.
Wheat cake flour and egg — contains gluten.
Fresh sashimi is naturally gluten-free — the served soy sauce contains wheat, so bring/request tamari for dipping.
Wheat-flour tempura batter — contains gluten.
Wheat-flour/starch coating — contains gluten.
Breaded/wheat-battered Hiroshima fish cake — contains gluten.

items.ja: コース料理（2時間飲み放題付）
items.note: Course includes several fried/tempura items (wheat). Ask in advance for sashimi and salt-grilled fish in place of battered/soy-sauce dishes, and bring tamari.

items.ja: おまかせコース（ディナー）
items.note: Seasonal tasting menu of Hiroshima/Yamaguchi produce. Reservation-based — phone ahead to explain celiac needs so the chef can swap out bread and flour-thickened sauces and use naturally gluten-free preparations.
Same kitchen; discuss gluten-free / vegan requirements in advance and the chef can accommodate.

hiro_tenshinkaku.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Note: a ~550 yen entrance/facility fee applies at this cafe.

items.note: Black specialty drip coffee — gluten-free and vegan.
Lemon soda — gluten-free and vegan.
Plain tea — gluten-free; served without milk unless requested.
Adds a cake (cheesecake or Hiroshima lemon cake) to a drink; cakes contain wheat.
Contains wheat (biscuit base/flour) and dairy — contains gluten.
Wheat cake flour — contains gluten.

items.ja: 精進料理（デザート・お抹茶付）
items.en: Shojin (Buddhist vegan) set with dessert & matcha
items.note: Fully plant-based. Shojin cooking can use wheat gluten (fu) and soy sauce — ask which dishes are wheat-free and request tamari instead of soy sauce.

items.ja: 精進カレー（副菜・ドリンク付）
items.note: Vegan, but Japanese curry roux normally contains wheat flour — contains gluten unless they confirm a flour-free version; ask.

items.en: Cold buckwheat soba, signature Shougen type, with dipping broth
items.note: The shop is 生粉/十割 (kiko/juwari): all noodles are 100% buckwheat with NO wheat binder (tsunagi) added — a Michelin Bib Gourmand pure-soba house, so the cooking water carries only buckwheat (low cross-contamination). The catch is the dipping tsuyu, which contains soy sauce (wheat). ASK to eat the cold noodles with salt only, or bring your own tamari; skip the tsuyu. Tsuyu also has bonito dashi, so not vegan as served.

items.en: Cold buckwheat soba, Sarashina (refined white) or Inaka (rustic) type
items.note: Also 100% buckwheat, no wheat binder. Same approach: request salt or use own tamari instead of the soy-based tsuyu.

items.en: Stacked bowls of cold 100% buckwheat soba (3 tiers)
items.note: Same wheat-free noodles served in stacked bowls; toppings/tsuyu poured over. ASK for salt and plain noodles, own tamari, and no soy-based tsuyu. 5-tier ¥2,640.

items.en: Buckwheat soba with grated mountain yam
items.note: Grated yam is GF; noodles are wheat-free. Served in/with soy-containing broth — ASK for a cold preparation with salt/own tamari rather than the soy tsuyu.
Noodles themselves are 100% buckwheat, but the hot broth is built on soy sauce (wheat) and cannot be removed — avoid the hot-broth dishes; choose a cold mori/wariko and control the dip instead.
Tempura is wheat-flour battered and fried — not GF and a fryer cross-contamination source. The soba is wheat-free, but order it plain (mori) without tempura.

Tandoori marinade is normally yogurt + spices + lemon (no soy, usually no wheat), so grilled tandoori chicken is a strong ASK item. Confirm no wheat flour or asafoetida (hing, often cut with wheat) is in the spice mix, and that it is not sharing the tandoor with naan dough.
Same yogurt-spice marinade as tandoori chicken. ASK to confirm no wheat/hing and separate cooking from naan.

Most North-Indian gravies are onion-tomato-spice based and naturally wheat-free, but ASK: some kitchens add a pinch of flour or asafoetida (hing) that contains wheat. Served with rice this is a good celiac ask; pair with plain/turmeric rice, not naan.
Vegetable curry can be vegan and wheat-free — ASK about ghee/cream (for vegan) and about flour/hing (for GF). Order with rice.

Pakora batter is besan (chickpea/gram flour), which is naturally gluten-free. ASK whether the batter is pure besan (some add wheat) and whether the fryer is shared with samosa/wheat items. Confirm oil vs ghee for vegan.

items.ja: ターメリックライス／ライス
items.note: Plain rice / turmeric rice (rice + turmeric, no soy or wheat) is safe as served. Ask for rice in place of naan with any curry set. The 若草ランチ set (2 curries + turmeric rice + naan + drink) is ¥1,230 — swap the naan for extra rice.
Papad is usually lentil or gram flour (GF), but seasoning often includes asafoetida (hing) commonly cut with wheat. ASK before eating.
Wheat-flour pastry shell — not gluten-free. Avoid; also a fryer cross-contamination source for pakora.
Wheat — not gluten-free. Substitute rice.

Wheat-flour batter, wheat noodles and soy-based okonomi sauce — NOT gluten-free. Important: the whole restaurant grills on a teppan coated with okonomiyaki batter, so every teppanyaki item below carries real cross-contamination risk. A celiac must ASK for a freshly cleaned section or foil.
Already salt-seasoned (塩焼き) rather than sauced, so no soy — a good ASK item. Request it be grilled on a clean part of the teppan/on foil, away from okonomiyaki batter.

items.en: Grilled offal — choice of tare sauce or salt
items.note: Menu explicitly offers a salt option — CHOOSE 塩 (salt), not タレ (tare, which is soy/wheat). Ask for a clean grill surface.
Classic ask-to-make-safe dish: request it stir-fried with salt (and oil), NOT soy sauce, or use your own tamari. Confirm no soy in the base and a clean grill area.
Plain grilled yam is naturally GF/vegan — ASK for salt only (no soy glaze) and a clean grill surface.
Whole small fish grilled with salt — no batter or sauce. ASK for salt only and a clean grill section.
Scallop + butter is GF, but butter-grilled dishes here are often finished with a splash of soy — ASK for salt/butter only, no soy sauce, on a clean surface.
Plain chilled tomato, no cooking and no sauce — safe as served and avoids the teppan entirely.
Boiled, salted soybeans — gluten-free and vegan as served; a safe no-grill starter.
Finished with soy sauce (wheat) — not GF as listed. Could in principle be asked salt-only, but ordered as-is it contains soy; choose もやし炒め or 焼やまいも with salt instead.
Wheat noodles + soy sauce — not gluten-free. Avoid.

items.ja: ゆみちゃんのおばんざいランチ
items.en: Yumi-chan's obanzai lunch (10+ seasonal vegetable small dishes, rice and soup)
items.note: Home-style set of 10+ small seasonal vegetable dishes plus rice — a textbook ASK situation. The owner notes the dishes change daily, so ask on the day which small plates use soy sauce/mirin/dashi and request those be left off or served salt-seasoned. Many simmered/pickled obanzai use soy (wheat); the plainer grilled/steamed/salted vegetable dishes are the ones to ask for.
Plain freshly-cooked rice — naturally gluten-free and vegan as served.
Egg rolled with dashi — the dashi/seasoning frequently contains soy sauce or mirin (wheat/alcohol). ASK how it is seasoned before eating.
Breaded with panko (wheat) and deep-fried — not gluten-free despite the salt-koji marinade. Avoid.
Tsukudani is simmered in soy sauce (wheat) — not gluten-free. Vegan but not GF.

Chef's rotating organic Japanese set (rice + vegetables + often fish). Plain rice is safe; ask them to season vegetables/fish with salt (塩) and to use tamari (たまり) not regular soy sauce, and to skip any flour-battered/simmered-in-soy items. For vegan, ask about bonito dashi, egg and fish.
Japanese-style curry roux almost always contains wheat flour — treat as wheat unless staff confirm a flour-free roux. Ask about dashi/dairy for vegan.
Same curry — Japanese curry roux normally contains wheat flour.
Smoked-curry set — same wheat-flour roux concern as above.
Lemon, sugar and soda — naturally gluten-free and vegan.

items.ja: 広島レモンジンサイダー
items.note: Distilled gin (local Sakurao) + lemon + soda. Distilled spirits are gluten-free even if grain-based.
Rum, lemon/lime, mint, soda — gluten-free and vegan. Seasonal April–November.
Gin + tonic water — gluten-free.
Beer is brewed from barley/wheat — not gluten-free. (Confirm vegan; some beers use animal finings.)

hiro_furaido_furaidou_momijid.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Per-item prices not published online; confirm in shop.

items.note: Just roasted sweet potato — naturally gluten-free and vegan.

items.ja: 国産焼き栗 くり香
items.note: Plain roasted chestnuts — naturally gluten-free and vegan.

items.ja: 焼き芋ソフトクリーム
items.note: Contains dairy (not vegan). Soft-serve mix can contain barley malt or wheat and the cone is wheat — ask for ingredients and request it in a cup, no cone/toppings.
Butter-cream sandwiched in a baked wheat-flour cookie — contains wheat.

items.ja: 選べるメインのビュッフェランチ
items.note: Lunch is a main + salad/soup buffet. Free bread is wheat. See individual mains below; a celiac should skip the pasta/gratin mains and ask whether the special plate can be made without wheat.
Wheat pasta.
Gratin béchamel is thickened with wheat flour.

items.ja: 今週のスペシャルプレート
items.note: Contents change weekly — ask if it can be prepared without wheat (no flour-dusting/sauce thickener/bread).

items.ja: 食べるスープ トマトクリームポタージュ
items.note: Potage may be thickened with flour and often contains cream — ask about wheat and dairy.
Vegetables are fine; ask about the dressing (soy sauce/wheat) and request no croutons. Ask for oil + salt or vinegar dressing.
Chiffon cake is made with wheat flour.

hiro_shimalabo.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Course dishes are examples and rotate seasonally.

items.ja: ランチコース（広島の恵み）
items.note: Organic/natural French, chef's course. Inform them of celiac when booking; ask to omit flour thickeners (roux), bread, and any flour-dusted items so the course can be built gluten-free.

items.ja: ディナーコース（自然の恵み ル・メニュー・デュ・テロワール）
items.romaji: dinner course (shizen no megumi / Le menu du terroir)
items.en: Dinner tasting course (Le menu du terroir)
items.note: Multi-course terroir tasting. Book ahead with celiac notice; the kitchen can tailor courses — ask them to avoid flour thickeners, bread and flour-dusting.

items.ja: 瀬戸内 海の幸ブイヤベース風
items.note: Seafood in tomato/saffron broth is often naturally gluten-free — confirm no flour and served without bread/rouille.

items.ja: 広島牛イチボのロースト コンソメ餡掛けトリュフ
items.romaji: Hiroshima-gyu ichibo no roast, consomme ankake truffle
items.en: Roast Hiroshima-beef rump, consommé sauce & truffle (course example)
items.note: Plain-roasted beef is fine; ask whether the consommé sauce is thickened with flour and request no flour-dusting.

As served the anago is glazed in sweet tare (soy sauce = wheat) and the set includes miso soup (usually wheat-containing dashi/miso). Plain white rice is safe. One GF-diner review reports the shop is 'very accommodating' — ask for anago grilled with salt only (no tare) over plain rice, and skip the miso; confirm directly before relying on it.

items.ja: 天ぷら定食（あなご天付き）
items.note: Standard tempura batter is wheat and dipping tsuyu contains soy sauce (wheat). A single unverified GF review reports the shop can make 'gluten-free tempura' on request — confirm this directly by phone/in person before ordering; do not assume.

hiro_indo_ryori_rasoi_hondori.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Per-item prices not fully published; lunch buffet price confirmed.

items.note: Buffet includes assorted curries and naan (wheat). A celiac can eat rice + wheat-free curries — ask staff which curries have no wheat/atta and avoid the naan.
Fermented rice-and-lentil crepe — naturally gluten-free. Ask for oil not ghee for vegan; confirm no wheat added.
Steamed rice-and-lentil cakes — naturally gluten-free and vegan.
Basmati rice with spices is generally gluten-free — confirm no wheat in the masala; contains ghee/meat (ask for vegan).
Tomato-cream curry; usually wheat-free but ask (some kitchens use flour/besan to thicken). Contains dairy — not vegan.
Ask whether the curry base uses any wheat thickener; over 15 curries and 5 spice levels are offered.
Yogurt-and-spice marinade is normally wheat-free — confirm no wheat in the marinade/coloring.
Naan is made from wheat flour — not gluten-free. Order rice instead.

items.ja: オーガニックブッダボウル
items.note: Rice-and-vegetable bowl, all-vegan. Dressing/seasoning may use soy sauce (=wheat); ask for salt/olive-oil dressing or bring own tamari. Confirm no wheat-based mock-meat topping.
Soy-meat marinated in organic 醤油 (soy sauce) and typically wheat-flour/batter coated — contains wheat. Not readily fixable as fried in shared batter.
Uses 国産小麦麺 (domestic wheat noodles) plus soy-based tare — contains wheat. No gluten-free noodle option confirmed.
Made with 国産有機小麦 (organic wheat flour). Bakery is wheat-based; breads/baked goods are not gluten-free.

hiro_nihon_ryori_kodama.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Course-only, reservation required; service charge added (和室 10%).

items.note: Omakase kaiseki (7 dishes at ¥7,000+, 9 dishes higher); dishes not published in advance. Celiac MUST phone ahead: chef can prepare sashimi (bring own tamari), salt-grilled fish (塩焼き), and steamed/simmered items without soy/tare, and omit tempura and soy-simmered courses. All ingredients natural/seasonal.

items.ja: 夜のおまかせコース 11,000円
items.note: 9-dish omakase; no fixed dish list. Arrange celiac needs at reservation — sashimi with own tamari, salt-grilled fish, plain rice, and non-soy preparations are the negotiable path; avoid tempura, soy/tare-glazed and soy-simmered dishes.

items.ja: 夜のおまかせコース 13,200円
items.note: Same negotiation as above — call ahead so the chef substitutes salt (塩) preparations and tamari-served sashimi for soy-sauce/tare dishes.

items.ja: 夜のおまかせコース 16,500円
items.note: Top premium course; celiac handled only by advance arrangement (sashimi + own tamari, salt-grilled fish, plain rice, no soy/wheat).

items.ja: 平日限定 日替わりランチ
items.note: Onigiri + main + sides + miso soup. Buildable gluten-safe: choose the plain salted onigiri (銀シャリ), skip the fried main, pick salt/vinegar sides, and confirm miso soup dashi/soy. Ask the salt-rice onigiri be made without furikake.

items.en: Nitaya onigiri set (2 onigiri, main, 2 sides, miso soup, coffee/juice)
items.note: Same build-your-own approach: plain-salt/umeboshi onigiri are safe; avoid fried mains (oyster fry, tatsuta) and soy-simmered sides; confirm miso soup.

items.ja: おにぎり 銀シャリ
items.note: Rice + salt (+ nori) only — gluten-free and vegan as served. The safest celiac choice; the shop's rice is the signature. Ask no furikake/seasoning added.
Salted pickled plum + rice — normally gluten-free and vegan. Confirm the ume has no soy-sauce/bonito seasoning.
Ikura is usually cured in soy sauce (醤油漬け = wheat). Ask if the roe is salt-cured; otherwise not gluten-free.

items.ja: おにぎり 辛子高菜
items.note: Takana pickle is commonly seasoned with soy sauce — ask about soy/wheat; treat as not GF unless confirmed.

items.ja: おにぎり 穴子かば焼き
items.note: Kabayaki tare is soy sauce + wheat, integral to the dish — contains wheat, not fixable.
Soy-sauce marinade (=wheat) and flour/starch coating fried in shared oil — contains wheat.
Panko-breaded and deep-fried — contains wheat.

items.ja: さば竜田の甘酢あんかけ
items.note: Flour-dredged fried fish plus soy/starch sweet-vinegar sauce — contains wheat.

items.en: Rolled omelette with spicy cod roe
items.note: Dashimaki egg often uses soy/mirin; mentaiko frequently contains wheat/soy in its cure — treat as containing wheat.
Tofu dressing usually seasoned with soy sauce and dashi (bonito). Ask for salt/sesame-only version to make GF and vegan.
Simmered in soy sauce (=wheat) and contains chicken — not gluten-free or vegan.
Rice-vinegar based; dressing may include a little soy sauce and dashi. Ask for vinegar-and-salt only.
Japanese mayo is usually GF but check brand; contains egg (not vegan). Confirm no wheat-containing seasonings.
Some miso contains barley/wheat and dashi is usually bonito. Ask for wheat-free (rice/soy) miso and kombu dashi to make GF/vegan.

items.ja: 砂肝と鶏ハツのコンフィサラダ
items.romaji: sunagimo to torihatsu no konfi salada
items.note: Confit meat itself is gf; ask for a plain salt/oil/vinegar dressing with no soy sauce or mustard.
Contents vary; ask for oil/vinegar or salt dressing (no wheat-containing dressing). Shop also lists a separate Vegetarian & Vegan menu, so a vegan version is likely.

items.ja: 旬のお魚のパピヨット
items.note: Fish steamed in parchment with vegetables/herbs; ask for no flour-thickened sauce and no soy — salt, herbs, olive oil, wine only.

items.ja: 瀬戸内ムール貝のマリニエール
items.note: Mariniere is wine, shallots, parsley, butter — usually gf, but ask for no flour thickening and to be served without the bread/baguette.
Ask for salt-and-pepper grilling with no soy-based or flour-thickened sauce and no bread garnish.

items.ja: サンドイッチセット（自家製食パン）
items.note: Made with house wheat shokupan (incl. an-butter and Hiroshima chicken versions) — contains gluten, not adaptable.

items.ja: 牡蠣のペペロンチーノ
items.note: Wheat pasta — contains gluten.
Dairy; Basque cheesecake typically contains a little wheat flour — treat as not gf unless confirmed flourless.

items.ja: 季節のフルーツタルト（津田いちご／古江いちじく等）
items.en: Seasonal fruit tart (Tsuda strawberry / Furue fig, etc.)
items.note: Wheat pastry crust — contains gluten.

Set changes daily — built on organic/brown rice with miso soup and seasonal no-pesticide vegetable dishes. Ask that vegetables be seasoned with salt/oil not soy sauce, and confirm none of the day's dishes use wheat.
Plain rice — gluten-free.
Made with pure organic RICE miso (wood-barrel aged, no additives) — rice miso is typically gluten-free; confirm the dashi contains no wheat (and is not bonito-based, for vegan).
No-pesticide/organic seasonal vegetables; ask for salt/oil seasoning rather than soy sauce to keep it gluten-free.
Wheat-flour cake — contains gluten.

items.ja: グルテンフリー豆腐ハンバーグプレート
items.note: Chicken-and-tofu patty made with no binder (no breadcrumbs/flour); served with organic sprouted brown rice, mini salad and choice of sides. Entire shop is gluten-free & additive-free. Confirm the sauce is tamari/miso-based.

items.ja: グルテンフリーガパオ（鶏と豆腐）
items.note: Gluten-free version — seasoned without standard soy/oyster sauce. Contains chicken.

items.ja: グルテンフリーチキンカレー
items.note: Made from scratch with domestic ingredients, no wheat roux, additive-free.

items.ja: 寝かせ酵素玄米（発芽玄米）
items.note: Plain fermented brown rice base — gluten-free and vegan.

items.ja: 日替わりプレートランチ
items.note: Daily plate built on enzyme brown rice; whole kitchen is gluten-free & additive-free. Ask for a vegan build.
Black brewed coffee is naturally gluten-free and the safest order here. If you add milk/cream that is a dairy (not gluten) issue — for vegan ask for it black.
Lime, syrup and soda water — no wheat. Gluten-safe.
Wheat bread — contains gluten. Not fixable. This is the only hot-food category besides hot sandwiches.
Wheat bread — contains gluten. Not fixable.

hiro_yuba_to_tofu_no_mise_ume.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. À la carte prices sourced from Ume no Hana's standardized chain menu (Umeda-store listing); Hiroshima店 items are essentially identical but prices may differ slightly.

items.note: Base is GF but Mineoka tofu is made with dairy milk. Usually served with wasabi-shoyu — ask to serve plain or bring own tamari. Soy sauce = wheat.
Sesame + kudzu starch, GF ingredients. Often served with a dab of soy sauce/wasabi — ask for it plain or dip in own tamari.
Same as sesame tofu — GF base; ask to skip soy sauce or use own tamari.

items.en: Tuna with yuba and grated yam
items.note: Raw tuna, yuba and tororo are GF, but comes with a soy-based sauce. Ask to serve without sauce and use own tamari.

items.ja: 湯葉豆腐とたぐり湯葉のお造り
items.romaji: yuba doufu to taguri yuba no otsukuri
items.note: Yuba (soymilk skin) is itself gluten-free. Danger is the dipping soy sauce (wheat). Bring/ask for tamari and it is GF.
Dashi custard is normally seasoned with wheat-containing soy sauce; not reliably fixable. Contains egg.
Wheat-flour dumpling wrapper — contains gluten. Not fixable.
House signature. Yuba lifted from hot soymilk is GF; served with a soy dipping sauce. Ask for plain or dip in own tamari.
Broth is kombu water (GF) and tofu is GF. The tsuke-dashi/ponzu dip contains wheat soy sauce — bring own tamari.
Plain cold tofu is GF; comes with soy sauce/ginger. Use own tamari.

items.ja: 湯豆腐と豚の豆乳しゃぶしゃぶ
items.romaji: yudoufu to buta no tounyuu shabushabu
items.note: Soymilk broth itself is GF, but ponzu and sesame dipping sauces contain wheat soy sauce — bring tamari. Contains pork.
Yuba is GF; dips contain wheat soy. Use own tamari.

items.ja: 黒毛和牛のしゃぶしゃぶ
items.note: Kombu broth GF; ponzu/goma dips contain wheat soy — bring tamari. Contains beef.
Warishita simmering sauce is soy sauce + mirin (wheat). Not fixable as cooked.

items.ja: ふく福豆冨 二色あん掛け
items.note: Ankake sauces are typically built on wheat soy sauce/dashi; not reliably fixable.
Battered/coated and deep-fried — contains wheat. Not fixable.
Nama-fu IS wheat gluten — inherently full of gluten. Never GF.

items.ja: はかた地どりの唐揚げ
items.note: Wheat-flour coating plus soy marinade. Not fixable.
Wheat spring-roll wrapper. Not fixable.
Panko (wheat) breading. Not fixable.

items.ja: 湯葉カニクリームコロッケ
items.note: Wheat béchamel + panko. Not fixable.
Panko-breaded skewers — wheat. Not fixable.
Wheat tempura batter. Not fixable.
Béchamel (wheat flour). Not fixable.
Panko/flour binder plus a soy-based sauce. Not reliably fixable.
Saikyo miso is white rice miso (usually GF), but confirm no soy sauce added to the marinade. Grilled fish, no batter. Contains fish.
Ask for salt (塩) seasoning only, no tare/soy dipping sauce — then GF. Contains beef.
Takikomi rice is cooked with soy sauce (wheat). Ask for plain white rice (白ご飯) instead; check pickles and skip miso soup if unsure.

items.ja: 豆乳仕立ての野菜ゴロゴロカレー
items.romaji: tounyuu jitate no yasai gorogoro karee
items.note: Japanese curry roux is thickened with wheat flour. Not fixable.
Eel kabayaki tare is soy sauce/mirin (wheat). Not fixable.
Wheat noodles. Never GF.
Rice gratin with wheat béchamel. Not fixable.

items.en: Soymilk ice cream with brown-sugar syrup
items.note: Soymilk ice + kuromitsu are typically GF; confirm no wheat/barley in the ice cream (some contain malt). If plain, safe.
Wrapper is wheat gluten (fu). Never GF.

hiro_primevere.price_note: Prices approximate — may change. Most drink prices not published; budget stated as ¥500+.

items.ja: ひろしまミックス（ミックスジュース）
items.en: Hiroshima Mix — signature fresh seasonal-fruit juice
items.note: House signature; fresh seasonal fruit slow-juiced to order, nothing added. Naturally gluten-free and vegan.
Freshly squeezed fruit only. GF and vegan.
Shiso, sugar and citric acid/vinegar drink — no wheat. GF and vegan.
Lemon + soda. GF and vegan.
Lemon, sugar, water. GF and vegan.
Lemon + dairy milk. Gluten-free but not vegan (milk).
Lemon, banana + dairy milk. GF but not vegan.

items.ja: サンド de おにぎり（穴子・牡蠣 など）

items.en: Meat-wrapped rice ball on a stick
items.note: Glazed with a teriyaki-style tare (soy sauce = wheat). Not fixable; contains meat.

items.ja: 新鮮トマトのカプレーゼ
items.note: Tomato, mozzarella, basil, olive oil — naturally gluten-free. Confirm no crouton/bread garnish.

items.ja: 広島産カキとほうれん草炒め
